mission.title: keylime: Improve tpm2.verify_ek to speed up cert matching
mission.description: Improve tpm2.verify_ek to speed up cert matching. Requested by 1+ users.

steps.title: Check current keylime deployment
steps.description:
Verify your keylime version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keylime -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keylime
helm list -n keylime 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working keylime installation.

steps.title: Review keylime configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant keylime configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n keylime -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keylime
kubectl get configmap -n keylime -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keylime
```
Currently we are brute forcing the entire list of signing certs by TPM manufacturers: 
  see https://github.com/mit-ll/python-keylime/blob/master/keylime/tpm2.py#L625

for signer in trusted_certs:
    signcert =

steps.title: Apply the fix for Improve tpm2.verify_ek to speed up cert matching
steps.description:
Extract the issuer (CA) from the EK certificate, then search for the matching subject in the entire list of signing certs by TPM manufacturers. If found verify the EK certificate against this CA.

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keylime -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keylime
kubectl get events -n keylime --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Improve tpm2.verify_ek to speed up cert matching" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Extract the issuer (CA) from the EK certificate, then search for the matching subject in the entire list of signing certs by TPM manufacturers. If found verify the EK certificate against this CA.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with keylime installed or the issue environment reproducible.
